title: Remainder %
subtitle: Leftover after division
goal: Understand how the remainder operator % works.
explanation: The % operator returns the remainder after division.
It shows what is left after a number is split into equal parts.
If there is no remainder, the result is 0.
The % operator is often used to check whether a number is even or odd.
It is one of the most useful math operators.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine packing 7 apples into boxes of 2. After three full boxes, one apple is left over. That is what the % operator returns.

remember: If there is no remainder, the result is 0.
% is often used to check even or odd numbers.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Many people think % means percent. In JavaScript, it is the remainder operator after division.
